summary: Deterministic app/runtime tests cover generated-artifact head drift, review-input packet boundaries, and eval-plan host-boundary behavior for the claim discovery workflow helper packet slice.

covered: Generated-only claim artifact drift remains visible as head drift while the claim-source stale flag stays fresh enough for review and eval planning.

The review-input helper emits a bounded cautilus.claim_review_input.v1 packet with clusters, skipped clusters, skipped claims, action-bucket focus, evidence carry-forward, and no LLM/application side effects.

The eval-plan helper emits a cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1 packet from reviewed cautilus-eval claims, skips non-ready or already satisfied claims, and keeps fixture, prompt, runner, wrapper, and policy authoring outside the product boundary.
notClaimed: This bundle does not prove live reviewer quality or host-owned fixture correctness.
This bundle does not claim that every future adapter mode can execute; it covers the checked-in helper packet contracts and regression tests.

outputSummary: passes review prepare flow through deterministic packet creation only
fails when review prepare overruns into apply-result or eval planning
accepts natural Codex and Korean reviewer boundary wording

checkedInEvidence.proves: CLI-level claim status, review-input, stale-packet, and eval-plan packet behavior.
Runtime builder-level review packet selection, skipped-claim evidence context, action-bucket focus, eval-plan eligibility, product-runner proof classification, and satisfied-claim exclusion.

tests: passes review prepare flow through deterministic packet creation only
fails when review prepare overruns into apply-result or eval planning
checkedInEvidence.proves: Operational review-prepare guidance stops at deterministic packet creation and does not drift into review application or eval planning.

decision.reason: The checked-in CLI/runtime tests and review-prepare audit exercise the exact packet contracts, stale/fresh boundary, selection policy, skipped-claim context, and host-boundary constraints asserted by the three claim-discovery workflow claims.
